rationale: The two evaluations describe an identical candidate, with only the name changed from a male, Eastern-European-coded name to a female, Western-coded name. The substance of the reasoning is essentially the same — both cite GISCollective entrepreneurship, full-stack fluency (DLang, Node, cloud), teaching/community leadership as strengths, and the same gaps (no AI/ML product leadership, no cloud platform strategy, only a BS). The strengths and concerns are reworded but materially equivalent, so the prose differences are justified noise. However, the score dropped from 6/10 to 5/10 for the same accomplishments with no job-relevant change, and the variant's framing is subtly more critical ('Limited evidence of advising strategic customers'). An unexplained one-point penalty tied solely to swapping in a female name reflects bias toward the demographic signal.
